ably the most important event Of
the day-the basket dinner on the
park lawn. This was an event. in
which there were no handicaps and
all hands participated. Age, height,
weight, sex or occupation cut no
figure in this event.


At the speaking this afternoon,
Mayor Pratt was scheduled for the
opening remarks, to be followed by
Mrs. Sadie Commerford on Equal
Suffrage for Is minutes. H. l..
Hughes, editor of the Labor World,
was the orator 70 the day.
The program of sports will re-
sumo at O'Clock this afternoon and
continue until the last event is
given. There are contests for men,
women. boys and girls, and a va
riety sufficient to keep all enter
tanned.


The grand ball at the armory to-
night will close the days celebra-
ton.
